perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for a period of one year, after which it automatically renews for successive one-year terms, unless revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the agreement has an initial term of one year with automatic renewals, unless revoked. The original text states the agreement remains in effect until revoked. This contradicts the condition of remaining effective as of the date in the agreement. This adds confusion as it is uncertain if the agreement is valid for a year or until revocation. The contradiction appears in the section regarding the term and revocation of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Tallgrass Energy, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for a period of one year, after which it automatically renews for successive one-year terms, unless revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating the agreement has a one-year term with automatic renewals unless revoked, which conflicts with the contradicted text, which also states the agreement has a one-year term with automatic renewals unless revoked. This creates an in-text contradiction by placing the same clause in two different locations, which can lead to confusion about the enforceability of the clause and whether the contradicted section is still applicable. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction,' as the clause's placement in two locations leads to conflicting implications and uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; furthermore, each party is jointly and severally responsible for the accuracy of all information contained in the filings, regardless of who provided it.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that each party is responsible for the accuracy of *all* information, contradicting the previous clause which states that parties are *not* responsible for the accuracy of information provided by others unless they know or have reason to believe it is inaccurate. This creates uncertainty regarding the scope of liability and due diligence required. The contradiction appears in the responsibilities section of the joint filing agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Tallgrass Energy, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; furthermore, each party is jointly and severally responsible for the accuracy of all information contained in the filings, regardless of who provided it. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating that each party is jointly and severally responsible for the accuracy of all information contained in the filings, regardless of who provided it. This directly conflicts with the original text (and the Contradicted Text, which contains the original text), which states that parties are not responsible for the accuracy of information provided by others unless they know or have reason to believe it is inaccurate. This creates a clear and impactful inconsistency regarding the scope of liability and due diligence required, directly impacting the enforceability of the agreement. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, however, only the first fully executed version submitted shall be considered the operative agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for any number of counterparts to form the agreement. The altered text, establishes only the *first* fully executed version as the operative agreement. This adds ambiguity in case if a counterpart is signed later with new modification. The contradiction appears at the last line of the first paragraph of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Tallgrass Energy, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, however, only the first fully executed version submitted shall be considered the operative agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause regarding counterparts. The original text stated that 'This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.' The changed text adds a limitation by specifying 'only the first fully executed version submitted shall be considered the operative agreement'. This creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text, containing the changed text, now stipulates that only the first version is operative, contradicting the implied allowance for all counterparts to be equally valid.
